data.title: 10 high-profitability small-cap stocks that are outperforming in 2026
data.description:
A notable shift is underway in the small-cap market in 2026: profitable Russell 2000 companies are decisively outperforming their non-profitable counterparts, with profitable stocks up approximately 8% year-to-date while non-profitable names are slightly in the red, a sharp reversal from 2025, when non-profitable small caps surged +20% versus only +10% for profitable ones. 

This flight to quality within the small-cap universe reflects a more discerning market environment, where investors are increasingly rewarding companies with real earnings rather than speculative growth stories.

Against this backdrop, the following list highlights the top 10 small-cap stocks with high Seeking Alpha Profitability Quant Grades of A- or better, ranked by their year-to-date performance, spanning technology, healthcare, industrials, and beyond, as a quality-first lens on the small-cap opportunity set

The list is topped by Spire Global (SPIR), with a standout YTD performance of 187.47%. Power Solutions International, Inc. (PSIX) and Blue Bird Corporation (BLBD) follow as the immediate runners-up, with gains of 35.88% and 34.15%, respectively.

The top ten features a diverse range of industries, including Puma Biotechnology, Inc. (PBYI) in the biotech sector and Mitek Systems, Inc. (MITK) in application software. While all stocks on the list carry strong profitability ratings, their Quant ratings vary from “Hold” to “Buy.”

Here is the list:

  1. Spire Global, Inc. (SPIR), YTD perf: 187.47%, Quant rating: 4.33

  2. Power Solutions International, Inc. (PSIX), YTD perf: 35.88%, Quant rating: 2.76

  3. Blue Bird Corporation (BLBD), YTD perf: 34.15%, Quant rating: 3.33

  4. Puma Biotechnology, Inc. (PBYI), YTD perf: 30.76%, Quant rating: 3.12

  5. Mitek Systems, Inc. (MITK), YTD perf: 28.44%, Quant rating: 3.35

  6. Viemed Healthcare, Inc. (VMD), YTD perf: 27.99%, Quant rating: 3.90

  7. BK Technologies Corporation (BKTI), YTD perf: 27.42%, Quant rating: 3.00

  8. Mesa Laboratories, Inc. (MLAB), YTD perf: 22.09%, Quant rating: 3.29

  9. Pebblebrook Hotel Trust (PEB), YTD perf: 20.32%, Quant rating: 3.02

  10. Westlake Chemical Partners LP (WLKP), YTD perf: 19.26%, Quant rating: 3.09

Small-Cap ETFs: (IJR), (IWM), (VB), (VBR), (VBK), (SCHA), (SPSM), and (DFAS)

data.title: Perella Weinberg discloses stock consideration for Gleacher Shacklock deal
data.description:
Perella Weinberg Partners (PWP) disclosed the stock consideration for its Gleacher Shacklock acquisition in an SEC filing on Monday after market hours.

PWP had agreed to acquire 100% of the membership interests of the London-headquartered independent advisory firm.

Perella Weinberg Partners is set to issue class A common stock to sellers of the membership interests as a portion of the purchase consideration.

The share consideration consists of 1,127,529 shares to be issued at closing; 2,255,058 additional shares to be issued in three annual tranches on each of the first, second, and third anniversaries of closing; and a contingent consideration based on fees received from certain client engagements.

A portion of the contingent consideration may be payable in shares, calculated based on the volume-weighted average trading price of common stock.

data.title: PC stocks fall after Nvidia denies report in talks to buy a PC-focused company (update)
data.description:
Update 4:35pm: Adds Nvidia denial.

Nvidia (NVDA) denied a report that it's in talks to buy a PC company that earlier boosted shares of PC makers Dell (DELL) and HP Inc. (HPQ)

“The media report is false; Nvidia is not engaged in discussions to acquire any PC maker,” a company spokesperson told Bloomberg News.

SemiAccurate earlier on Monday reported that Nvidia (NVDA) was in negotiations for over a year to acquire a large company, and it will reshape the personal computer landscape, SemiAccurate reported.

The news outlet noted that it has been following the story since late 2024, adding that the time is approaching to make a deal or walk.

Shares of PC makers: Dell Technologies (DELL) surged about 6%, while HP (HPQ) jumped around 4% following the report. 

Dell, and HP didn't immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

data.title: FedEx CFO John Dietrich to step down with FedEx Freight spin-off
data.description:
FedEx Corp. (FDX) chief financial officer John Dietrich will step down on June 1, corresponding with the completion of the FedEx Freight (FDXF) spin-off to a separate publicly traded company.

Claude Russ, vice president of finance, will serve as interim CFO until a permanent successor for Dietrich can be found.

Dietrick will remain at the company until July 31. 

Additionally, FedEx (FDX) confirmed its FY26 guidance for a profit of $19.30 to $20.10 per share on a 6% to 6.5% increase in revenue.

For the freight segment, revenue is expected to reach $8.7B in 2026, with adjusted operating income of ~$1.1B and adjusted operating margin of ~12%.

Looking further out to 2029, FedEx (FDX) is expected to achieve $8B in operating income and an operating margin of 8%, with an expectation to expand operating margins by 200 basis points compared to midpoint FY26. GAAP earnings are targeted at $25 per share, excluding FedEx Freight (FDXF).

FedEx (FDX) shares closed modestly lower Monday and are unchanged in after-hours trading.

data.title: 3 things to look out for on Tuesday
data.description:
U.S. equities closed higher on Monday even as U.S.-Iran negotiations broke down and Washington moved toward a naval blockade of major Iranian ports. 

On the session, the tech focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) surged +1.2%, outperforming the S&P 500 (SP500), which closed higher by +1%, and the Dow (DJI) which added +0.6%.

Now, here are three focus points for investors on Tuesday:

  • Traders will be closely watching the upcoming Producer Price Index report, as markets look for fresh signals on pipeline inflation and cost pressures at the wholesale level. The data will be parsed for evidence of cooling input prices or lingering inflation, which could influence expectations for Federal Reserve policy and broader market direction.
  • Market participants will also be focused on a fresh wave of bank earnings, with BlackRock (BLK), Wells Fargo (WFC), JPMorgan Chase (JPM), and Citigroup (C) all set to report before the opening bell. Results will be closely analyzed for trends in net interest income, loan demand, and capital markets activity, offering insight into the health of the financial sector and broader economic conditions.
  • Investors will further be watching earnings from Johnson & Johnson (JNJ), with investors looking for insight into performance across its pharmaceutical and medical technology segments. The report will be closely examined for guidance on revenue growth, margin trends, and any updates on product pipelines that could influence sentiment toward the broader healthcare sector.

data.title: AllianceBernstein AUM slips 4.7% in March
data.description:
AllianceBernstein's (AB) assets under management fell to $839B as of March 31, 2026, from $880B at the end of February, primarily due to market depreciation, the company said on Monday.

To a lesser extent, net outflows also contributed to the decline during the month. Modest net inflows in Private Wealth were offset by net outflows across Retail and Institutional channels, it said.

For the quarter ended March 31, 2026, preliminary firmwide net outflows were $7.2B.

Total equity AUM of $327B at March 31 fell 7.9% from Feb. 28. That compared with the S&P 500's decline of 5.1% during the month.

Fixed income AUM of $312M fell 2.8%, while alternatives/multi-asset solutions AUM of $200B edged down 2.0% M/M.

data.title: Net Power appoints Lee Shuman as new CFO
data.description:
• Net Power (NYSE: NPWR) announced on Monday that it had appointed Lee Shuman as CFO, effective April 13, 2026.
  • Shuman succeeds Rice, who had served as interim CFO since April 2025 and will remain principal financial officer for SEC reporting purposes through the filing of the company’s quarterly report for the period ended March 31, 2026.
  • After that, Shuman will assume the role of principal financial officer, while Rice will continue as CEO.
  • Shuman previously served as Head of Power Finance at Javelin Global Commodities and CFO of WattBridge Energy, bringing over 25 years of experience and having led more than $10B in power-related transactions.
  • As CFO, he will oversee the company's financing strategy with a focus on project-level funding.
  • Shares +1.32%.

data.title: Copa Holdings reports 15% rise in March passenger traffic
data.description:
• Copa Holdings (CPA) on Monday said that its system-wide passenger traffic rose 15.3% year-on-year in March, outpacing a 14.8% increase in capacity.
  • The airline reported revenue passenger miles of 2,623.5 million for the month, while available seat miles reached 3,025.7 million.
  • Load factor, a measure of seat occupancy, edged up 0.4 percentage points to 86.7% compared with March 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Allogene Therapeutics announces public offering of $175M of common stock
data.description:
• Allogene Therapeutics (ALLO) announced on Monday that it intends to offer and sell $175M of shares of its common stock in an underwritten public offering.
  • All the shares are being offered by Allogene. In addition, Allogene intends to grant the underwriters for the offering a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional $26.25 million of the shares of its common stock offered in the public offering. 
  • Allogene expects to use the net proceeds from this offering for general corporate purposes, which may include clinical trial expenses, research and development expenses, general and administrative expenses, and capital expenditures.
  • Goldman Sachs & Co., Jefferies, and TD Cowen are acting as the joint book-running managers for the offering. TPG Capital BD is acting as co-manager for the offering.
  • ALLO -6.38% after hours to $2.86.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Chevron, Shell set to sign deals for oil and gas areas in Venezuela - Reuters
data.description:
Chevron (CVX) and Shell (SHEL) are expected to sign agreements that would significantly increase U.S. production of Venezuelan heavy crude and natural gas, Reuters reported Monday.

Chevron (CVX) is planning to sign an agreement that would return an offshore gas field to Venezuela and increase its operations in the country’s main oil region, while Shell (SHEL) is expected to sign an agreement to take control of the Loran gas field for operations, according to the report.

The U.S. company reportedly has been in ​talks to relinquish ⁠Loran, where it had confirmed more than 7 Tcf of reserves years ago but did not develop; it is now focused on expanding Petropiar, its main oil project in Venezuela, into the neighboring Ayacucho 8 area in the Orinoco Belt.

The agreements would be among the first big expansion deals since the U.S. captured Venezuela's President Maduro and the approval of a sweeping reform of the country's ​main oil law meant to encourage foreign investment.

The specific terms of the deal are not yet known, although they are expected to be signed in the presence of acting Venezuelan President Rodriguez.
